title: foundation — kill-switch flags
description: Kill-switches for foundation's infrastructure. All default ON — flipping to OFF is an incident-response action. Every change to these flags emits an audit event + a PagerDuty alert.

flags.description: Disables the ModuleRouteLoader — every module's routes stop responding. Used only during boot-time module-registration incidents. Effectively takes the app offline; use with care.
Disables GET /api/health aggregate probe walking. /api/ping + /up remain up. Used when an aggregate probe cascade is causing load spikes on downstream services.
Disables IndexSearchableJob dispatch on model saves. Used during search-engine incidents; search index goes stale until reindex is run. Existing queries keep working (stale results).

Disables HasAuditable's audit-row writes. Used only during audit-table incidents (corrupt schema, deadlock storm). Data mutation continues; audit trail is suspended. Every use recorded in the platform incident log.
Emits RFC 8594 Sunset + Deprecation headers on responses from deprecated /api/v* prefixes. Off in local dev to reduce log noise; on everywhere else.
Enforces the response envelope contract strictly — controllers returning raw arrays (not enveloped) cause a 500 in strict mode. Useful in test/staging to catch regressions; usually on in prod.

retirementPolicy.description: Kill-switches never retire — they exist for incident response. Rollout flags retire 90 days after reaching 100% GA per foundation's change management contract (SOC 2 CC8.1).
